原文: A housewarming party is a special party to be held when someone buys or moves into a new apartment or house. The person who bought the house or moved is the one who throws the party. The party is a chance for friends and family to congratulate① the person on the new home. E. It also gives people a chance to see what the new home looks like. And it is a good time to fill the new space with love and hopefully presents.
翻译: 乔迁派对是当某人购买或搬入新公寓或房子时举行的一种特殊聚会。购买或搬入房子的人就是举办派对的人。这个派对是朋友和家人祝贺此人乔迁新居的机会。它也让人们有机会看看新家是什么样子。而且，这是一个用爱和希望礼物填满新空间的好时机。
必背词汇:
① congratulate /kənˈɡrætʃuleɪt/ v. 祝贺，向…道贺
Ⓡ congratulation /kənˌɡrætʃuˈleɪʃn/ n. 祝贺，恭喜
Ⓡ congratulate sb. on sth. 为某事祝贺某人

原文: B. It is traditional to bring a gift to a housewarming party. Some people register② a list of things they want or need for their new home at a local store or stores. Some common things people will put on a gift registry③ include kitchen tools like knives and things like curtains. Even if there isn't a registry, a good housewarming gift is something to decorate④ the new house with, like a piece of art or a plant.
翻译: B. 带礼物参加乔迁派对是一种传统。有些人会在当地一家或多家商店登记一份他们为新家想要或需要的物品清单。人们通常会放在礼物登记表上的常见物品包括像刀具这样的厨房工具和像窗帘这样的东西。即使没有登记表，一份好的乔迁礼物也是用来装饰新房子的东西，比如一件艺术品或一株植物。
必背词汇:
② register /ˈredʒɪstə(r)/ v. 登记，注册
Ⓡ registration /ˌredʒɪˈstreɪʃn/ n. 登记，注册
Ⓡ registered /ˈredʒɪstəd/ adj. 已注册的
③ registry /ˈredʒɪstri/ n. 登记处；登记表
Ⓡ gift registry 礼物登记表
④ decorate /ˈdekəreɪt/ v. 装饰，装潢
Ⓡ decoration /ˌdekəˈreɪʃn/ n. 装饰，装饰品
Ⓡ decorative /ˈdekərətɪv/ adj. 装饰性的

原文: C. You can also bring food or drinks to share with the other guests. This is often appreciated⑤ since at a housewarming there isn't a lot of food served. There are usually no planned activities like games at a housewarming party. The host or hostess⑥ of the party will, however, probably give all the guests a tour of their new home. Sometimes, because a housewarming party happens shortly after a person moves into their new home, people may be asked to help unpack⑦ boxes. A. This isn't usual though.
翻译: C. 你也可以带食物或饮料与其他客人分享。这通常很受赞赏，因为在乔迁派对上通常不会提供很多食物。乔迁派对上通常没有像游戏这样的计划活动。然而，派对的主人可能会带所有客人参观他们的新家。有时，因为乔迁派对发生在一个人搬入新家后不久，人们可能会被要求帮忙拆箱。A. 不过，这不常见。
必背词汇:
⑤ appreciate /əˈpriːʃieɪt/ v. 欣赏；感激
Ⓡ appreciation /əˌpriːʃiˈeɪʃn/ n. 欣赏；感激
Ⓡ appreciative /əˈpriːʃətɪv/ adj. 感激的；欣赏的
⑥ host /həʊst/ n. 主人；主持人 v. 主办
Ⓡ hostess /ˈhəʊstəs/ n. 女主人
Ⓡ host a party 举办派对
⑦ unpack /ˌʌnˈpæk/ v. 打开（包裹等）；拆箱
Ⓡ pack /pæk/ v. 打包 n. 包裹
Ⓡ package /ˈpækɪdʒ/ n. 包裹

原文: Housewarming parties get their name from the fact that a long time ago people would actually bring firewood⑧ to a new home as a gift. G. This was so that the person could keep their home warm for the winter. Now most homes have central heating⑨ and don't use fires to keep warm.
翻译: 乔迁派对得名于很久以前人们实际上会带柴火作为礼物到新家的事实。G. 这是为了让那个人能在冬天保持家里温暖。现在大多数家庭都有中央供暖系统，不使用火来取暖。
必背词汇:
⑧ firewood /ˈfaɪəwʊd/ n. 柴火，木柴
Ⓡ fire /ˈfaɪə(r)/ n. 火 v. 点燃
Ⓡ wood /wʊd/ n. 木头；树林
⑨ heating /ˈhiːtɪŋ/ n. 供暖系统；加热
Ⓡ heat /hiːt/ n. 热 v. 加热
Ⓡ heater /ˈhiːtə(r)/ n. 加热器
